feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Orchestration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Orchestration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Configuration to expose WebService via HTTPS in SAP PO 7.5
entry.content:
Experts,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out to seek guidance on the necessary configuration to expose a WebService in SAP PO 7.5 through HTTPS instead of HTTP.

I have already developed the Service Interfaces and the channel to expose the WebService using the SOAP protocol. However, when generating the WSDL in Integration Builder, it is generated in the "normal" format, i.e., http://[host]:[port]/dir/wsdl?p=XXXXXXXX. My requirement is for this WSDL to be accessible via HTTPS, such as https://[host]:[port]/dir/wsdl?p=XXXXXXXX.

I would greatly appreciate it if someone could provide detailed information on the steps I need to follow or the configuration adjustments I should make in SAP PO 7.5 to achieve this secure exposure via HTTPS.

Thank you very much for your time and assistance!

Best regards.

entry.title: Exclusion mask(Regular Expression) issue in SFTP Sender channel
entry.content:
Exclusion mask issue in SFTP sender channel

I have a requirement, for 2 SFTP sender channels:

1) Select only files which do not contain the string TEST anywhere in the filename(Exclusion mask )

2) Select only files containing the string TEST anywhere in the filename(File name mask)

From same source directory I need to pick the files

For the 1St case I am using Exclusion mask to exclude files such as TEST1,TEST2,TEST3,TEST4 and TEST5 I have given all these in Exclusion mask by one by one

For the 2Nd case I have given TEST1 ,TEST2 under File name mask such as .*TEST1.*

FileName: .*(dot *)

.*TEST2.*

It is working well(File name mask). But Exclusion mask is not working as expected.

I tried with couple of regex expressions but none of these are working.

Filename: .*(dot*)

(?<!TEST1).*\\.*

^(?!.*TEST1).*

Please suggest me regular expression to exclude files.

Can someone please suggest what changes I need to make in Exclusion mask to avoid picking the files?

Thanks,

Vidya

entry.title: Redesign adapter modules in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using the following modules in SAP PO for a few interfaces.

  • MessageTransformBean
  • XMLAnonymizerBean
  • TextCodepageConversionBean
  • DynamicConfigurationBean and some other modules for Async- sync bridge & sync- Async bridge.

Could you please guide me to know what is the redesign approach in BTP CI for all the above standard adapter modules in SAP PO? Thank you!

 

Regards

Ramu.

entry.title: Receiver Mail adapter: xml to CSV file attachment
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I have a requirement where the input xml to be converted to CSV file and send this CSV as an attachment to the emails that are configured. we are using the receiver mail adapter and since FCC is not possible, need to use adapter modules and parameters in the receiver adapter. 

So If anyone is aware of this solution, please help me with the module beans and parameters to be used to achieve this.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Acknowledgement not possible error in Log viewer page
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement where i need to pick a file from SAP system and call a REST URL to download a .tar file in response and place it into AL11 folder. I have created a File receiver adapter to place the .tar file in AL11 using RequestResponseBean from File Sender channel. 
The .tar file is successfully being placed in AL11 folder and message is showing successful in message monitor but when i am checking the logs in Log viewer page, it is showing the below error:

"Cannot report error to audit log. AuditMsgKey is null
Acknowledgement handling failed for type SystemAck. Reason: com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.exception.MessagingException: Acknowledgement not possible for synchronous message
Acknowledgement not possible for synchronous message."

Kindly help me to resolve this error.

Thanks & Regards,

Niyati

entry.title: Relaunching Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh – Repackaging Summary
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce that we are launching a new and repackaged version of Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. The initial version of Advanced Event Mesh was launched back in 2022, and we were delighted by the interest and attention it garnered. In our endeavor to offer customer-centric services, we decided to incorporate all the feedback we had received since the initial launch of the service in 2022 and offer a refreshed version of Advanced Event Mesh that can help you in building real-time, event-driven landscapes. This refreshed plan also contains some key innovations that will enable you to build business processes that can help you gain a competitive edge.

All these new features and innovations will be available under the new “Standard” service plan.

For more information on pricing of these new SKUs, please visit the Discover Center. Read on to find out more…

 

 

What’s New?

Our guiding philosophy for this new offering was to keep the licensing as simple as possible while ensuring that all the key innovations and mission critical features are available across the board.

Reduced Number of SKUs

We have reduced the total number of SKUs from 10 to 8, by incorporating the dedicated region and customer private region in all the broker types. In addition, you have a new SKU to extend the memory spool of your brokers up to 6TB in increments of 500GB. Here’s the list of new SKUs:

  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, 100
  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, 250
  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, 1K
  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, 5K
  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, 10K
  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, 50K
  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, 100K
  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, spooling add-on

The number attached to each broker size indicates the number of client connections that it can support. For more details on the technical capabilities of each broker, refer to the official documentation on help.sap.com.

New Features and Innovations

As mentioned earlier, all brokers get the dedicated and private region deployment options included. You also get distributed tracing with the newly launched brokers, enabling the admin user to track the lifecycle of an event though the Event Mesh. For more information on distributed tracing, see the official documentation.

In addition to all this, you can also extend the message spool size of each broker in increments of 500GB by purchasing the spooling add-on. This message spool size will be available in addition to the already included spool memory with each broker. For example, the Enterprise 1K broker comes with 50GB of message spool. If you purchase and assign the entitlement for 2 units of the spooling add-on, you will get an additional 1TB of message spool, giving you a total message spool size of 1050GB.

Your Choice of Licensing Model

The new Advanced Event Mesh SKUs will be available on both subscription and consumption based pricing models, meaning you will be able to activate the new brokers on Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement (CPEA) and Pay-as-you-Go (PayGo) licensing models in addition to the standard subscription-based licensing. This, combined with the hourly broker pricing gives you full flexibility to use the brokers as per your requirement and optimize your total cost of ownership (TCO).

 

What happens to the old brokers and pricing plan?

As of today, we are announcing that we will deprecate the old Advanced Event Mesh brokers on CPEA and Pay-as-you-go pricelists. Following a 6-month notice period, these older plans will be deprecated. Current CPEA and PayGo customers will be able to access both the old and new brokers till the end of your current contract period. After your current contract is renewed, the brokers which you have activated from the older plans will continue to run till you deactivate them. However, we strongly recommend that you consider migrating to the newly launched SKUs owing to all the advantages and benefits that it offers.

We hope you are as excited as we are to experience the new Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. We strive to deliver solutions that can help you digitally transform your enterprise to be future-ready and gain a competitive edge. We look forward to any feedback and thoughts you have on this new launch via the comments section of this post.

entry.title: Inbound proxy HTTP 500 Internal Server Error SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on HTTP(Axis) to Proxy(SOAP-XI) synchronous scenario in SAP PO 7.5 single stack.

Only some of the requests are getting back response messages and remainig are getting failed with following error like shown in screenshot below. But, we do not get error(but only delay in secs) while trying to send same failed request messages from SPROXY tcode of backend. But, issue is occuring in such a way only some of the synchronous requests are getting failed in SAP PO.

Could you please guide to resolve this issue and let us know how to find root cause of this issue.

Regards

Ramu

entry.title: Token from REST response HTTP header SAP PI
entry.content:
SAP Process Integration 

I have a requirement on Proxy to REST service. The rest service is accessible after token is retrieved from another rest service.

Proxy - > REST TOKEN service (token returned as http header) -> REST Service (token passed as http header) - > Proxy

I am unable to use REST Look up as http header is not returned in Look up.. is there any other way to accomplish this?

Kindly share your thoughts.

entry.title: SOAP Axis Adapter - How To Activate Log Viewer Trace?
entry.content:
I am developing a SOAP to AXIS scenario in SAP PI.

The Axis receiver is configured with WSS4J for SOAP signature. I need to see the SOAP Envelope after the signature has been applied, but I am not able to do it.

I have tried with XPI Inspector, Wireshark adn TCPMon.

I have seen that is possible to get really cool debug information from the Axis and XML Security methods in the NWA Log Viewer as this blog screenshots shows: SOAP(axis) receiver scenario - signature verificat... - SAP Community

But when looking for those locations in the Log Configuration, none of them are available:


Is there anything I need to do to be able to collect those logs?

entry.title: SAP GRC NFE NT 2024 001 (CTe 4.00)
entry.content: Poderia nos ajudar a entender quando será lançado alguma SAP Note referente a NT 2024 001 (CTe 4.00)

entry.title: PIPO file pick from AL11
entry.content:
(A) SAP S4 AL11 directory contains some data dumps, like CSV files. 

(B) SAP PIPO will pick these csv files from the AL11 directory on time intervals ( A --> B ),is this possible ? if so how ?

(C) SAP PIPO process the content and save the file to FTP site ( B --> C ) .

B to C is fine , A to B is it possible ? and how to do so ?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: How SAP PI/PO can read flat files from SAP S4 , in the AL11 directory ?
entry.content: How SAP PI/PO can read flat files from SAP S4 , in the AL11 directory ?

entry.title: AXIS Adapter - Edit Log Configuration Locations
entry.content:
I am following the SAP Note 1039369 FAQ XI Axis Adapter.

Q: Which components are relevant for generating a debug trace output?

I am looking to be able to trace WSS4J in an Axis scenario with XML signature. The FAQ document explains that I need to add entries to the log-configuration.xml in the Axis SDA.

However, I don't know where is that file located. I have tried placing the example file provided in the FAQ in the META-INF folder of the com.sap.aii.af.axisproviderlib.sda and redeploy it to PI, but that doens't seem to work.

Besides, the Trace Locations in the NWA seems broken:

That <unknownclass> entry is very weird but I don't really know if it was there before.

I am not able to find any proper explanation about the XML file and how to add those entries.

entry.title: RFC lookup in case of backend maintenance SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on synchronous interface with RFC lookup(calling Function module on SAP Backend) like shown in below picture. Based on the RFC lookup output value received from SAP S4, the incoming request has to be either forwarded to system B or system C & get back the response.

This should be ideal scenario when SAP S4 is up and running like shown below. There is a mapping defined with RFC lookup in this case and this scenario works perfectly fine.

But, now in case of when SAP S4 is down during maintenance mode, the incoming request should be always forwarded to System B. In that case, we have also defined different mapping to be executed without RFC lookup & The corresponding response received from System B should be send back to System A.

Is it possible to execute one mappings when system is up and another mapping during maintenance window.

How SAP PO system will get to know when there is SAP S4 in maintenance mode?

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Third party URL hitting
entry.content:
Hi team,

Actually we have one requirement I have third party URL so how to configure in sap po to get the data in sap system I checked URL in postman so i get vehicle details but through sap po how to trigger blank data like i have to hit the URL so i will get all details in sap PO if any lead would help me it would be great I m new to sap po so please it's really urgent and if any one created same scenario please share snap with me.

 

Thanks

entry.title: REST adapter - additional header parameters
entry.content:
All,

I have an additional requirement in a SAP PO REST receiver adapter. 2 parameters must be provided in the header

  • Content-Length
  • Host

For the host, I can provide a fixed value. But what about the Content-Length. That one is calculated within Postman.

Any pointers or ideas?

Many thanks.
Dimitri

entry.title: ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for..
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am facing the following issue with establishing a connection using a RestReceiver. Unfortunately, there is an issue with the certificate. The following error is appearing:

ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm!

ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm!

There is an SAP note regarding this matter. It explains:

"Contact the administrators of the target server to create a certificate signed with the ECDSA encryption algorithm." 

3057117 - Peer-Zertifikat von ChainVerifier abgelehnt - EC-signiertes SHA256withRSA-Serverzertifikat Serverzertifikat nicht für ECDHE_ECDSA-Schlüsselaustauschalgorithmus geeignet! - SAP for Me

However, are there any other solutions available?

On the internet, it is mentioned that it is indeed possible to use a SHA256withRSA signature with the ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm.

Do you have any insights?
I would greatly appreciate your input.

Best Regards,
Mohammad

entry.title: Cancel job in SAP PO 7.5 is not considering 4 year old messages
entry.content:
Hi Friends, 

Good day.

Our PIPO system version is 7.5.

I try to schedule a cancel job to cancel 4-year-old messages. (There are huge messages are in holding state in 2020). When I scheduled this job, I entered Older Than (Hours) parameter values as 35040. (365 * 4 * 24). However, the job is not considering 4-year-old messages it seems. After successful job run, the job log says, "Number of "EOIO" messages to be cancelled: 0 (in 0 sequences). 2020 holding messages are not cancelled. 

Entered value for parameter Process Only Messages with Status as "TBDL, WAIT, HOLD, NDLV". 

Could you please clarify why the job is not cancelling 2020 messages?

Thank you. 

Regards,

Jegatheeswaran P.

entry.title: Merging Multiple PDF Attachments into a Single PDF in SAP PI/PO Using Java Mapping
entry.content:
Hello!

Today I will try to explain how we can merge multiple PDF attachment input into single PDF.

In SAP Process Integration/Process Orchestration (PI/PO), efficient handling of document attachments is crucial for streamlined business processes. Often, there's a need to consolidate multiple PDF attachments into a single PDF file for easier management and distribution. In this blog, we'll explore how to achieve this seamlessly using Java Mapping.

Brief requirement: The real life scenario demanded to send a bundle of different third party system generated PDF as an attachment to SAP PI/PO . The multiple PDF should be merged together and send as an merged output to another third party application. 

  1. Understanding Input and Output:

    • Input: Multiple PDF attachments in the message payload.
    • Output: Single merged PDF file.

  2. Java Mapping Implementation:

    • Create a new Java Mapping program in SAP NetWeaver Developer Studio or SAP Integration Suite.
    • Import necessary libraries for PDF handling (e.g., iText).
    • Implement logic to:
      • Iterate through the input PDF attachments.
      • Extract content from each PDF attachment.
      • Append the content to the output PDF file.

 

import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream;
import java.io.File;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.nio.file.Files;
import java.util.Base64;
import java.util.Collection;

import org.apache.pdfbox.multipdf.PDFMergerUtility;
import org.apache.pdfbox.io.*;

import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.AbstractTransformation;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.Attachment;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.InputAttachments;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.OutputAttachments;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.StreamTransformationException;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.TransformationInput;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.TransformationOutput;
//import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.exception.AttachmentNotFoundException;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.MessageDirection;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.MessageKey;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.PublicAPIAccessFactory;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.auditlog.AuditAccess;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.auditlog.AuditLogStatus;

public class PDFMergingUtility extends AbstractTransformation {

    @Override
    public void transform(TransformationInput transformationInput, TransformationOutput transformationOutput)
            throws StreamTransformationException {

        final String DASH = "-";
        String msgID;
        String uuidTimeLow = "";
        String uuidTimeMid = "";
        String uuidTimeHighAndVersion = "";
        String uuidClockSeqAndReserved = "";
        String uuidClockSeqLow = "";
        String uuidNode = "";
        String msgUUID = "";
        AuditAccess audit;
        MessageKey msgKey;

        InputAttachments inputAttachments = transformationInput.getInputAttachments();
        OutputAttachments outputAttachments = transformationOutput.getOutputAttachments();

        try {
            msgID = transformationInput.getInputHeader().getMessageId();
            uuidTimeLow = msgID.substring(0, 8);
            uuidTimeMid = msgID.substring(8, 12);
            uuidTimeHighAndVersion = msgID.substring(12, 16);
            uuidClockSeqAndReserved = msgID.substring(16, 18);
            uuidClockSeqLow = msgID.substring(18, 20);
            uuidNode = msgID.substring(20, 32);
            msgUUID = uuidTimeLow + DASH + uuidTimeMid + DASH + uuidTimeHighAndVersion + DASH + uuidClockSeqAndReserved
                    + uuidClockSeqLow + DASH + uuidNode;
            msgKey = new MessageKey(msgUUID, MessageDirection.INBOUND);
            audit = PublicAPIAccessFactory.getPublicAPIAccess().getAuditAccess();
            audit.addAuditLogEntry(msgKey, AuditLogStatus.SUCCESS, "Java Mapping: Started");

            if (inputAttachments != null && inputAttachments.areAttachmentsAvailable()) {
                Collection<String> collectionIDs = inputAttachments.getAllContentIds(true);
                PDFMergerUtility pdfMerger = new PDFMergerUtility();

                for (String attachmentID : collectionIDs) {
                    Attachment attachment = null;
                    try {
                        attachment = inputAttachments.getAttachment(attachmentID);
                        String contentType = attachment.getContentType();

                        if (contentType.contains("pdf")) {
                            File tempFile = File.createTempFile("temp", ".pdf");
                            Files.write(tempFile.toPath(), attachment.getContent());
                            pdfMerger.addSource(tempFile);
                        }
                    } catch (Exception e) {
                        audit.addAuditLogEntry(msgKey, AuditLogStatus.WARNING,
                                "Attachment not found: " + attachmentID);
                    }
                }

                ByteArrayOutputStream mergedPDFStream = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
                pdfMerger.setDestinationStream(mergedPDFStream);
                pdfMerger.mergeDocuments(null);

                Attachment mergedAttachment = outputAttachments.create("MergedDocument.pdf",
                        mergedPDFStream.toByteArray());

      //Writing merged document as an attachment.
                outputAttachments.setAttachment(mergedAttachment);
                audit.addAuditLogEntry(msgKey, AuditLogStatus.SUCCESS, "Java Mapping: Merged PDF created");
            } else {
                audit.addAuditLogEntry(msgKey, AuditLogStatus.WARNING, "No attachments found");
            }
        } catch (Exception exception) {
            throw new StreamTransformationException(exception.toString());
        }
    }
}

entry.title: Process Integration test tool SAP PO usages for migration to BTP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

As you know we have a tool called Process Integration test tool in SAP PO.

We are planning to migrate the interfaces from SAP PO to Cloud Integration capability of Integration Suite.

Does that tool can be used while regression testing of migrating interfaces from SAP PO to Cloud Integration?

May I know the replacement of such tool in Cloud Integration capability of Integration Suite?

 

Thanks,

Ramu.
